metadata.title:
TRACTION co. DECLARES


FOR. OPEN SHOP POLICY

Superintendel.. informs the Carmen
That They Must Break Up


Their Union.

pages.raw_text:
AS the members of the street
carmen's union understand 1t, the
Traction Co. has declared for an
open snop and intends to abolish
the union organization on It's sys-
TEN.


According to union carmen
about 20 Of them were called into
Supt. Fairs Office yesterday and
informed that the union was do
ing nobody any good and they
would De expected to have nothing
more to do with it. Seventeen OF
the 20 are reported as asserting to
quitting the union in preference to
losing their jobs.


Tomorrow afternoon at 2 o'clock
the street carmen Will meet with
Richard Cornelius, one Of the inter.
national officers, and another meet.
ing will be held in the evening. The
outcome of these meetings is ex.
pected to be fight to maintain the
union, which may mean a strike
The union has membership OF
about $50 and 1t Is expected that
at least 100 Of these will be willing
to sustain the two thirds vote ne
cessary to authorize strike.


A number of carmen are cited as
victims already of the companys
lately adopted nonunioh policy
among them being a former finan
cial secretary, who was told fatty
that he was too active in union af
fairs. George Durand, president OF
the union, Is not now employed by
the Traction Co., having quit. 9th
err active union men have been
weeded out through spies, 1t Is
claimed


The new scale which the com
pany offered as compromise &c
the unions demand, and which re
stores the reduced pay the union
submitted to during the stringency
last winter. goes into effect today
1t also carries an overtime pro
vishl of some benefit to the day
men. But the union must go, as
the men interpret the superinten
dents latest announcement. Nc
